name: Manannán mac Lir Extended
description: Manannán mac Lir, meaning 'Manannán son of the Sea', is a prominent sea god in Irish mythology, deeply associated with the Otherworld and magic. He is depicted as a powerful sorcerer, navigator, and shapeshifter, ruling over the seas and the mystical island of Emain Ablach, a paradise often identified with the Isle of Man. Manannán possesses a vast array of magical items, including a self-steering boat named Scuabtuinne (Sea-Sweeper), a cloak of invisibility called Féth Fíada, and a horse named Enbarr, which can travel over land and sea. He is a protector of heroes and a guardian of knowledge, often intervening in mortal affairs to guide or test individuals. Beyond his maritime dominion, Manannán is also connected to the realms of death and rebirth, symbolizing the cyclical nature of life. He is revered for his wisdom, his mastery of the elements, and his ability to traverse the boundaries between worlds. Some accounts portray him as a benevolent figure, while others emphasize his potent and sometimes unpredictable magical abilities. He is a complex and multifaceted deity, representing the untamed power and mystery of the ocean, the allure of the Otherworld, and the enduring legacy of Celtic lore. His influence extends beyond the sea, impacting the fate of heroes, the course of battles, and the very fabric of the mythological landscape. Manannán's role as a psychopomp, guiding souls to the afterlife, further solidifies his importance in the Celtic understanding of life and death. He embodies the liminal spaces, the transitions, and the unseen forces that shape the world.
subtitle: Sea God, Sorcerer, Guide to the Otherworld

keyMyths.title: Manannán's Cloak
keyMyths.description: Manannán's cloak of invisibility, the Féth Fíada, allows him to move unseen, protecting him and those he chooses to shield. This cloak symbolizes his power to manipulate perception and access hidden realms.

keyMyths.title: The Sea-Sweeper
keyMyths.description: Scuabtuinne, Manannán's self-steering boat, navigates the seas without a crew, reflecting his mastery over the ocean. It highlights his independence and connection to the unpredictable nature of the sea.

keyMyths.title: Enbarr the Horse
keyMyths.description: Enbarr, Manannán's horse, travels effortlessly over land and sea, symbolizing his ability to transcend boundaries. The horse represents his freedom and connection to both the mortal and Otherworldly realms.

sources.author: Patricia Monaghan
